descEn: for loops and if/else — real Python power!

teacherGuide.conceptGu: for loop = same action repeat. if/else = decision. range(5) = 0,1,2,3,4. Indentation = Python rule — spaces matter! Loop + condition = AI algorithm foundation.
teacherGuide.conceptEn: for loop iterates, if/else decides. Indentation is Python syntax. These two concepts power almost all programs.
teacherGuide.storyGu: 'for i in range(5): print(i) = 0,1,2,3,4 print! Loop 5 times! Robot clean 5 rooms = for loop! if score > 10: print("Winner!") = game logic!'
teacherGuide.scriptGu: 'Try in replit: for i in range(3): print("Hello Yuval!") — watch Hello print 3 times! Magic!'
teacherGuide.tipsGu: 💡 replit.com — type for loop together! See output 3 times = wow moment! Indentation with TAB key — show it matters!

questions.textGu: for i in range(3): — loop _____ times run?
questions.textEn: How many times does: for i in range(3) run?
if score > 10: print("Win!"). If score=8, what prints?
questions.hintGu: 8 > 10? No! So if block skip — nothing prints!
questions.hintEn: 8 is NOT greater than 10, so condition is False — nothing prints!

questions.textGu: if age >= 18: print("Adult") else: print("Child"). age=7, output?
questions.textEn: if age >= 18: print("Adult") else: print("Child"). age=7?
questions.hintEn: 7 is NOT >= 18, so else runs: Child!

questions.textGu: for i in range(3): print("Hi!") — total output lines?
questions.textEn: for i in range(3): print("Hi!"). How many lines print?
questions.hintEn: Loop runs 3 times: Hi! Hi! Hi!

questions.textGu: AI training = data ઉપર loop. 1000 images train = ?
questions.textEn: AI training loops over data. Training on 1000 images uses a ___?
questions.hintGu: for image in dataset: train! Loop over all images = AI training!
questions.hintEn: for loop over all training data — that's how AI learns!
